label: Dense vector retrieval

node.label: Dense vector retrieval
node.definition: Dense retrieval embeds queries and candidates into vector space and finds approximate nearest neighbors under a learned similarity function.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Dense vector retrieval is to connect External retrieval & ranking, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Hybrid retrieval & reranking, Vector databases & ANN indexes, Retrieval-augmented generation.
examples: substrate for Vector databases & ANN indexes
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include External retrieval & ranking, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: Dense vector retrieval uses External retrieval & ranking as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Dense vector retrieval stores, selects, or obtains information through Embeddings & distributed representations.
Dense vector retrieval uses Contrastive and mutual-information objectives as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
